skill.name: pubchem-database-skill
skill.description: Programmatic access to the PubChem database (via PUG-REST API and PubChemPy) for searching chemical compounds, retrieving physicochemical properties, performing structure similarity/substructure searches, and obtaining bioactivity data.
skill.summary: Search PubChem for chemical compounds, retrieve physicochemical properties, and obtain bioactivity data through Python scripts and the PUG-REST API.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 54 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner misidentified markdown code blocks in SKILL.md as shell backtick executions (38 findings), and flagged legitimate PubChem API URLs as hardcoded network calls (7 findings). The skill only communicates with the official PubChem API at pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov. No malicious intent, obfuscated code, or data exfiltration was found. The skill legitimately uses the requests library and PubChemPy to query a public scientific database.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL to PubChem API
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The URL points to pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov, the official PubChem API endpoint. This is the intended data source for this scientific database query skill. The script uses requests.get() with a 10-second timeout to fetch bioactivity data by compound CID.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs flagged as hardcoded URLs
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The api_reference.md file contains documentation URLs for PubChemPy and PUG-REST. These are reference links for developers, not executable network calls.

low_findings.title: Markdown code blocks flagged as shell command execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The SKILL.md file contains markdown fenced code blocks (```bash) that show example commands for users. These are documentation examples, not executable backtick commands. They show users how to run Python scripts for compound searches.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance flag on standard API access
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The scanner flagged get_compounds() and URL construction as reconnaissance. These are standard PubChemPy operations for querying a public chemical database. No unauthorized system access is performed.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm flag in YAML front matter
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The scanner flagged SKILL.md line 3 which contains a YAML description field. No cryptographic algorithm exists anywhere in the file. This is a scanner error.

dangerous_patterns.title: External network requests to PubChem API
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill makes HTTP GET requests to pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov using the requests library in scripts/pubchem_ops.py. This is the intended and necessary behavior for querying the PubChem chemical database. All requests use HTTPS and have a 10-second timeout.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell command execution examples in documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md contains markdown code blocks showing shell commands for running Python scripts. These are documentation examples only and are not automatically executed. Users must manually run these commands.

content.user_title: Search and analyze the PubChem chemical database
content.value_statement: Chemical researchers and drug discovery teams need fast access to compound data spread across multiple databases. This skill provides a single Python interface to query PubChem for compound properties, structure searches, and bioactivity data through the PUG-REST API and PubChemPy library.
actual_capabilities: Search for chemical compounds by name, CID, SMILES, InChI, or molecular formula using PubChemPy
Retrieve physicochemical properties including molecular weight, LogP, TPSA, and IUPAC name
Perform similarity and substructure searches against the PubChem compound database
Fetch bioactivity assay summaries for specific compounds by CID via PUG-REST
Handle PubChem rate limits automatically (maximum 5 requests per second)
limitations: Requires network access to pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov and cannot work offline
Limited to data available in PubChem; does not search other chemical databases (e.g., ChEMBL, DrugBank)
PubChem rate limits (5 requests per second) may slow down large batch queries
Structure search performance depends on PubChem server response times for complex queries

use_cases.title: Retrieve compound properties for drug discovery
use_cases.description: A medicinal chemist needs molecular weight, LogP, and TPSA values for a set of candidate compounds to assess drug-likeness using Lipinski rules.

use_cases.title: Find similar compounds for hit expansion
use_cases.description: A researcher has a promising hit compound and needs to find structurally similar molecules in PubChem for analog screening and SAR analysis.

use_cases.title: Retrieve bioactivity data for target validation
use_cases.description: A biologist needs to check what bioassay data exists for a compound of interest to understand its known biological activity profile before proceeding with experimental validation.
use_cases.target_user: Biology researchers and assay development scientists

prompt_templates.title: Look up a compound by name
prompt_templates.prompt: Search PubChem for Aspirin and retrieve its molecular weight, LogP, and IUPAC name.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick lookup of basic chemical properties for a known compound

prompt_templates.title: Find similar compounds by SMILES
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the SMILES string CC(=O)OC1=CC=CC=C1C(=O)O to find the top 10 similar compounds in PubChem with a similarity threshold of 90 percent.
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding structural analogs for a known molecule

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve bioactivity data by CID
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the bioactivity assay summary for PubChem compound CID 2244 and list the target information and activity outcomes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Checking what biological assays are associated with a compound

prompt_templates.title: Batch property analysis for multiple compounds
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up PubChem properties for Ibuprofen, Paracetamol, and Naproxen. Compare their molecular weights, LogP values, and hydrogen bond donor counts to assess drug-likeness.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comparative analysis of multiple drug candidates

output_examples.input: Search PubChem for Aspirin and retrieve its properties.
Find similar compounds to Aspirin (CID 2244) with at least 90 percent similarity.
output: Found 12 compounds above 90 percent similarity threshold.

best_practices: Use compound names or SMILES strings for initial searches; convert to CID for bulk operations to improve reliability
Insert a 0.2-second delay between requests to stay within PubChem rate limits (5 requests per second)
Validate SMILES and InChI strings before submitting structure searches to avoid empty results
anti_patterns: Do not send more than 5 requests per second to PubChem; exceeding the rate limit may result in temporary IP blocking
Do not rely on PubChem as the sole data source for regulated research without cross-referencing other databases
Do not search using unsupported query types; only name, cid, smiles, inchi, and formula are accepted

faq.question: What data sources does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses the PubChem PUG-REST API and PubChemPy library to query the PubChem database hosted by the U.S. National Institutes of Health.

faq.question: What compound identifiers are supported for searching?
faq.answer: The skill supports search by compound name, PubChem CID, SMILES, InChI, and molecular formula.

faq.question: What properties can be retrieved for a compound?
faq.answer: Default properties include molecular weight, XLogP, TPSA, isomeric SMILES, and IUPAC name. The property list can be customized.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: No. The PubChem PUG-REST API is publicly available without authentication. No API key is needed.

faq.question: Are there rate limits on PubChem API requests?
faq.answer: Yes. PubChem limits requests to a maximum of 5 per second. The skill is designed to respect this limit.

faq.question: Can this skill search multiple compounds at once?
faq.answer: The skill processes one query at a time. For batch processing, you can loop over a list of compounds while respecting the 5 requests per second rate limit.
